tools.name: search_clinical_trials
tools.description: Search for clinical trials registered on clinicaltrials.gov based on title, conditions, interventions, outcome measures, and status. Returns a paginated list of studies, containing the NCT ID and description of each trial. You can then take the NCT IDs and use 'get_clinical_trials_*' tools to get detailed information about specific protocol fields for specific studies, or 'extract_clinical_trials_efficacy/safety' tools to get efficacy or adverse events results from specific studies. If you wish to see the next page of results, you can use the 'nextPageToken' value from the previous output of this tool and input it as the 'pageToken' parameter in the next query. Note that currently the search is limited to trials beyond phase 1.

condition.description: Query for condition or disease using Essie expression syntax (e.g., 'lung cancer', '(head OR neck) AND pain AND NOT "back pain"').

intervention.description: Query for intervention/treatment using Essie expression syntax (e.g., 'chemotherapy', 'immunotherapy', 'olaparib', 'combination therapy').

query_term.description: Query for 'other terms' with Essie expression syntax (e.g., 'combination', 'AREA[LastUpdatePostDate]RANGE[2023-01-15,MAX]', 'Phase II'). Can be used to search for all other protocol fields, including but not limited to title, outcome measures, status, phase, location, etc.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of studies to return per page (default 10, max 1000).

pageToken.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results, obtained from the 'nextPageToken' field of the previous response. Do not specify it for first page. When you make an initial request to the API which supports pagination, the response will include a nextPageToken. This token can then be used as a parameter in the subsequent API request to retrieve the next set of data.

tools.notes: type=ClinicalTrialsSearchTool; labels=ClinicalTrials.gov, Clinical Trial, Intervention, Adverse Effect, Efficacy, Search

tools.name: extract_clinical_trial_outcomes
tools.description: Extracts detailed trial outcome results (e.g., overall survival months, p-values, etc.) from clinicaltrials.gov, using their NCT IDs.

nct_ids.description: List of NCT IDs of the clinical trials (e.g., ['NCT04852770', 'NCT01728545']).

outcome_measure.description: Outcome measure to extract. Example values include 'primary' (primary outcomes only), 'secondary' (secondary outcomes only), 'all' (all outcomes), or specific measure names such as 'survival', 'overall survival'. For specific measure names, outcome measures will be matched as long as the input partially matches their titles or descriptions (case agnostic). Querying for specific measure names is recommended after getting an overview of outcome measures ('primary'). If querying for specific measure names does not return any results, this parameter should be set to 'primary' for sanity check. By default, the value is set to 'primary', i.e. the tool will extract all primary outcome results.

tools.notes: type=ClinicalTrialsDetailsTool; labels=ClinicalTrials.gov, Clinical Trial, Study, Outcome, Efficacy, Primary Outcome, Secondary Outcome

tools.name: extract_clinical_trial_adverse_events
tools.description: Extracts detailed adverse event results from clinicaltrials.gov, using their NCT IDs.

nct_ids.description: List of NCT IDs of the clinical trials (e.g., ['NCT04852770', 'NCT01728545']).

organ_systems.description: List of organs or organ systems to filter adverse events (see enum for exact text). Adverse events will be matched only if the input exactly matches their terms (case agnostic). If not specified, all adverse events will be returned. By default, all adverse events will be returned.

enum: Neoplasms Benign, Malignant and Unspecified (Including Cysts and Polyps)

adverse_event_type.description: Type of adverse events to extract. Options are 'serious' (serious adverse events only), 'other' (non-serious adverse events only), 'all' (all adverse events), or specific event names such as 'nausea', 'neutropenia', 'epilepsy' (from MedDRA). For specific event names, adverse events will be matched as long as the input partially matches their terms (case agnostic). Querying for specific adverse event names is recommended as there are typically many adverse events logged. If querying for specific event names does not return any results, this parameter should be set to 'serious' for sanity check. By default, the value is set to 'serious', i.e. the tool will extract all serious adverse events.

tissue.description: Tissue type for tissue-specific interactions. Examples: 'brain', 'heart', 'liver', 'kidney', etc.

max_node.description: Maximum number of nodes to retrieve in the interaction network. Warning: the more nodes, the more time it takes to retrieve the data. Default is 10 (~30 seconds).

interaction.description: Specific interaction type to filter by. Available types: 'co-expression', 'interaction', 'tf-binding', 'gsea-microrna-targets', 'gsea-perturbations'. If not specified, all types will be included.

string_mode.description: Whether to return the result in string mode. If True, the result will be a string of the network graph and the biological processes. If False, the result will be a NetworkX graph and a list of biological processes.

tools.name: openalex_literature_search
tools.description: Search for academic literature using OpenAlex API. Retrieves papers with title, abstract, authors, publication year, and organizational affiliations based on search keywords.

search_keywords.description: Keywords to search for in paper titles, abstracts, and content. Use relevant scientific terms or phrases.

max_results.description: Maximum number of papers to retrieve (default: 10, maximum: 200).

year_from.description: Start year for publication date filter (e.g., 2020). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published from this year onwards.

year_to.description: End year for publication date filter (e.g., 2023). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published up to this year.

open_access.description: Filter for open access papers only. Set to true for open access papers, false for non-open access, or omit for all papers.

tools.name: ScientificTextSummarizer
tools.description: Summarizes biomedical research texts, abstracts, or papers with specified length and focus areas. Uses AI to extract key findings, methodology, and conclusions from complex biomedical literature.

text.description: The biomedical text, abstract, or paper content to be summarized.

focus_area.description: What to focus on in the summary (e.g., 'methodology', 'results', 'clinical implications', 'drug interactions').

tools.name: CodeQualityAnalyzer
tools.description: Analyzes code quality from multiple dimensions including algorithmic correctness, functional implementation capability, performance characteristics, and best practices. Provides detailed feedback and improvement suggestions.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being analyzed

tool_description.description: Description of what the tool is supposed to do

tool_parameters.description: JSON string of tool parameters and their types

implementation_code.description: The actual implementation code to analyze

test_cases.description: JSON string of test cases for the tool

test_execution_results.description: JSON string of test execution results including pass/fail status and actual outputs

tools.name: MedicalLiteratureReviewer
tools.description: Conducts systematic reviews of medical literature on specific topics. Synthesizes findings from multiple studies and provides evidence-based conclusions with structured analysis and quality assessment.

research_topic.description: The specific medical/research topic for literature review (e.g., 'efficacy of drug X in treating condition Y').

literature_content.description: The literature content, abstracts, full studies, or research papers to review and synthesize.

focus_area.description: Primary focus area for the review (e.g., 'therapeutic efficacy', 'safety profile', 'diagnostic accuracy', 'biomarker validation').

study_types.description: Types of studies to prioritize in the analysis (e.g., 'randomized controlled trials', 'meta-analyses', 'cohort studies', 'case-control studies').

quality_level.description: Minimum evidence quality level to include (e.g., 'high quality only', 'moderate and above', 'all available evidence').

review_scope.description: Scope of the review (e.g., 'comprehensive systematic review', 'rapid review', 'scoping review', 'narrative review').

tools.name: HypothesisGenerator
tools.description: Generates research hypotheses based on provided background context, domain, and desired format. Uses AI to propose novel, testable hypotheses for scientific exploration.

context.description: Background information, observations, or data description from which to derive hypotheses.

domain.description: Field of study or research area (e.g., 'neuroscience', 'ecology', 'materials science').

hypothesis_format.description:
Optional directive on how to structure each hypothesis. Choose from one of the following formats:

1. If–Then Statements: "If [independent variable condition], then [expected outcome]."
2. Null and Alternative (Statistical):
   • H₀ (Null): "There is no difference/effect/association between X and Y."
   • H₁ (Alt): "There is a difference/effect/association between X and Y."
3. Associative (Correlation-Focused): "There is a relationship/association between [Variable A] and [Variable B]."
4. Directional (Non-If–Then): "Increasing/decreasing [Variable A] will lead to [directional change] in [Variable B]."
5. Comparative (Group Comparison): "Group A will show higher/lower [dependent measure] compared to Group B under [condition]."
6. Mechanistic: "Because [mechanism or process], [Variable A] will cause [Variable B]."
7. Descriptive (Exploratory/Pattern-Oriented): "Population X exhibits pattern Y in context Z."

If omitted, defaults to concise declarative sentences.

tools.name: ExperimentalDesignScorer
tools.description: Assesses a proposed experimental design by assigning scores and structured feedback on hypothesis clarity, variable definitions, sample size, controls, randomization, measurement methods, statistical analysis, bias mitigation, ethical considerations, and overall feasibility.

hypothesis.description: A clear statement of the research hypothesis to be tested.

design_description.description: A detailed description of the proposed experimental design, including variables, methods, sample details, and planned analyses.

tools.name: MedicalTermNormalizer
tools.description: Identifies and corrects misspelled drug or disease names, returning a list of plausible standardized terms.

raw_terms.description: A comma- or whitespace-separated string containing one misspelled drug or disease name.

tools.name: NoveltySignificanceReviewer
tools.description: Provides a structured peer-review of the work's originality and potential impact.

tools.name: LiteratureContextReviewer
tools.description: Reviews coverage, relevance, and critical synthesis of prior scholarship.

tools.name: MethodologyRigorReviewer
tools.description: Evaluates design appropriateness, sampling, and procedural transparency.

tools.name: DataAnalysisValidityReviewer
tools.description: Checks statistical choices, assumption testing, and reporting transparency.

tools.name: ResultsInterpretationReviewer
tools.description: Judges whether conclusions are data-justified and limitations addressed.

tools.name: WritingPresentationReviewer
tools.description: Assesses clarity, organization, grammar, and visual presentation quality.

tools.name: ReproducibilityTransparencyReviewer
tools.description: Evaluates data, code, and protocol availability for replication.

tools.name: EthicalComplianceReviewer
tools.description: Checks adherence to ethical standards and disclosure practices.

tools.name: QuestionRephraser
tools.description: Generates three distinct paraphrases of a given question while ensuring answer options remain valid and applicable.

question.description: The original question text to be rephrased

options.description: Answer options (e.g., multiple choice options) that should remain valid for the rephrased questions. Leave empty if no options are provided.

tools.name: ProtocolOptimizer
tools.description: Reviews an initial protocol and delivers targeted revisions that improve clarity, feasibility, risk-management, and evaluation rigor.

tools.name: TestCaseGenerator
tools.description: Generates diverse and representative ToolUniverse tool call dictionaries for a given tool based on its parameter schema. Each tool call should be a JSON object with 'name' (the tool's name) and 'arguments' (a dict of input arguments), covering different parameter combinations, edge cases, and typical usage. Can generate targeted test cases based on previous optimization feedback.

tool_config.description: The full configuration of the tool to generate test cases for. May include '_optimization_feedback' and '_iteration' fields for feedback-driven test generation.

tools.name: DescriptionAnalyzer
tools.description: Analyzes a tool's original description and the results of multiple test cases, then suggests an improved description that is more accurate, comprehensive, and user-friendly. Optionally provides a rationale for the changes.

original_description.description: The original description of the tool.

test_results.description: A JSON string containing a list of test case input/output pairs.

tools.name: ArgumentDescriptionOptimizer
tools.description: Optimizes the descriptions of tool arguments/parameters based on test case results and actual usage patterns. Provides improved descriptions that are more accurate and user-friendly.

parameter_schema.description: JSON string of the original parameter schema with properties and descriptions.

test_results.description: A JSON string containing test case input/output pairs showing parameter usage.

tools.name: DescriptionQualityEvaluator
tools.description: Evaluates the quality of tool descriptions and parameter descriptions, providing a score and specific feedback for improvements.

parameter_descriptions.description: JSON string of parameter names and their descriptions.

test_results.description: JSON string containing test case results.

tools.name: ToolSpecificationGenerator
tools.description: Generates complete ToolUniverse-compliant tool specifications based on a description and analysis of similar existing tools. Creates comprehensive tool configurations including parameters, prompts, and metadata.

tool_description.description: Brief description of the desired tool functionality and purpose.

similar_tools.description: JSON string containing configurations of similar existing tools for analysis and differentiation.

existing_tools_summary.description: Summary of existing tools in the ecosystem to avoid duplication and identify gaps.

tools.name: AdvancedCodeQualityAnalyzer
tools.description: Performs deep analysis of code quality including complexity, security, performance, and maintainability metrics with domain-specific expertise

source_code.description: The source code to analyze for quality assessment

analysis_depth.description: Level of analysis depth to perform

domain_context.description: Domain context for specialized analysis (e.g., bioinformatics, web development)

tools.name: ToolImplementationGenerator
tools.description: Generates domain-specific, functional code implementations based on tool descriptions and requirements with intelligent algorithm selection

tool_description.description: Detailed description of what the tool should accomplish

tool_parameters.description: JSON string of parameter schema for the tool

tools.name: ToolSpecificationOptimizer
tools.description: Optimizes tool specifications for clarity, completeness, and usability with comprehensive benchmarking against similar tools

tool_config.description: JSON string of current tool configuration to optimize

similar_tools.description: JSON string array of similar tools for comparison and benchmarking

tools.name: DomainExpertValidator
tools.description: Provides domain-specific validation and expert recommendations for tools with deep expertise across scientific and technical domains

tool_config.description: JSON string of tool configuration to validate

validation_aspects.description: JSON array string of specific aspects to validate

tools.name: ToolQualityEvaluator
tools.description: Evaluates the quality of tool configurations and implementations. Provides detailed scoring and feedback for improvement.

tool_config.description: JSON string of the tool configuration

evaluation_aspects.description: Aspects to evaluate (functionality, usability, completeness, best_practices)

tools.name: ToolOptimizer
tools.description: Optimizes tool configurations based on quality feedback. Improves tool specifications and implementations to address identified issues.

tool_config.description: JSON string of the original tool configuration

quality_feedback.description: JSON string of quality evaluation feedback

optimization_target.description: What to optimize for (improve_quality, enhance_performance, etc.)

tools.name: CodeOptimizer
tools.description: Optimizes code implementation for tools based on quality evaluation. Takes tool configuration and quality evaluation results to produce improved source code.

tool_config.description: JSON string containing the complete tool configuration including current implementation

quality_evaluation.description: JSON string containing quality evaluation results and feedback

tools.name: LabelGenerator
tools.description: Generates relevant keyword labels for tools based on their name, description, parameters, and category. Creates a comprehensive list of tags for tool discovery and categorization.

tool_description.description: Detailed description of what the tool does

tool_parameters.description: JSON string describing the tool's input parameters and their types

category.description: The general category or domain the tool belongs to

existing_labels.description: JSON array string of existing labels to consider reusing (optional)

tools.name: call_agentic_human
tools.description: Produces a concise, practical answer that emulates how a well-informed human would respond to the question.

question.description: The user's question to be answered in a human-like manner.

tools.name: ToolMetadataGenerator
tools.description: Generates a JSON structure with the metadata of a tool in ToolUniverse, given the JSON configuration of the tool.

tool_config.description: JSON string of the tool configuration to extract metadata from

tool_type_mappings.description: A mapping from a simplified toolType to a list of tool_config.type that fall under the toolType (e.g., {'Databases': ['XMLTool']})

tools.name: ToolMetadataStandardizer
tools.description: Standardizes and groups semantically equivalent metadata strings (e.g., sources, tags) into canonical forms for consistent downstream usage.

metadata_list.description: List of raw metadata strings (e.g., sources, tags) to standardize and group.

limit.description: If provided, the maximum number of canonical strings to return. The LLM will group terms more aggressively to meet this limit, ensuring all raw strings are mapped.

tools.name: ToolRelationshipDetector
tools.description: Analyzes a primary tool against a list of other tools to identify meaningful, directional data flow compatibilities for scientific workflows. Returns a list of compatible pairs with direction and rationale.

tool_a.description: JSON string for the primary tool configuration (Tool A).

other_tools.description: JSON string of a list of other tool configurations to compare against Tool A.

tools.name: drugbank_vocab_search
tools.description: Search the DrugBank vocabulary dataset for drugs by name, ID, synonyms, or other fields using text-based queries. Returns detailed drug information including DrugBank ID, common name, CAS number, UNII, and synonyms.

query.description: Search query string. Can be drug name, synonym, DrugBank ID, or any text to search for.

search_fields.description: Fields to search in. Available fields: 'DrugBank ID', 'Accession Numbers', 'Common name', 'CAS', 'UNII', 'Synonyms', 'Standard InChI Key'.

case_sensitive.description: Whether the search should be case sensitive.

exact_match.description: Whether to perform exact matching instead of substring matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: drugbank_vocab_filter
tools.description: Filter the DrugBank vocabulary dataset based on specific field criteria. Use simple field-value pairs to filter drugs by properties like names, IDs, and chemical identifiers.

condition.description: The type of filtering condition to apply. Filter is case-insensitive.

value.description: The value to filter by. Not required when condition is 'not_empty'. Examples: 'insulin' (for contains), 'DB00' (for starts_with), 'acid' (for ends_with), 'Aspirin' (for exact)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: drugbank_full_search
tools.description: Search the cleaned DrugBank dataframe (one row per drug) by ID, common name, or synonym. Returns identifiers, ATC, main pharmacology text fields, and protein partners. For best results, it is recommended that one uses `drugbank_vocab_search` to obtain DrugBank ID from other keywords first, and use this tool with DrugBank ID.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'DB00945', 'acetylsalicylic', 'Acarbosa').

search_fields.description: Columns to search in. Choose from: 'drugbank_id', 'name', 'synonyms'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Max number of rows to return.

tools.name: drugbank_links_search
tools.description: Search the cross-reference table linking DrugBank IDs to external identifiers (CAS, KEGG, PubChem, ChEBI, PharmGKB, UniProt, etc.) and web resources.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'DB00002', 'Cetuximab').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'DrugBank ID', 'Name', 'CAS Number', 'Drug Type', 'KEGG Compound ID', 'KEGG Drug ID', 'PubChem Compound ID', 'PubChem Substance ID', 'ChEBI ID', 'PharmGKB ID', 'HET ID', 'UniProt ID', 'Wikipedia ID', 'Drugs.com Link', 'NDC ID', 'ChemSpider ID', 'BindingDB ID', 'TTD ID'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

tools.name: dict_search
tools.description: Search the DICTrank dataset for drug-induced cardiotoxicity (DICT) risk information by trade name, generic name, or active ingredient. Searching with exact match is not recommeded.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'ZYPREXA', 'Olanzapine').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Trade Name', 'Generic/Proper Name(s)', 'Active Ingredient(s)'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

tools.name: dili_search
tools.description: Search the DILIrank dataset for drug-induced liver-injury (DILI) risk information by compound name. Searching with exact match is not recommeded.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'acetaminophen').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Compound Name'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

tools.name: diqt_search
tools.description: Search the DIQTA dataset for drug-induced QT-interval prolongation risk information by generic name or DrugBank ID. Searching with exact match is not recommeded for generic name.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'Astemizole', 'DB00637').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Generic/Proper Name(s)', 'DrugBank ID'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

tools.name: mcp_auto_loader_txagent
tools.description: Automatically discover and load all tools from TxAgent MCP Server. Can register discovered tools as individual ToolUniverse tools or provide tool configurations.

tools.name: mcp_auto_loader_expert_feedback
tools.description: Automatically discover and load all tools from Human Expert Feedback MCP Server. Provides tools for consulting human experts on complex medical decisions.

tools.name: AdverseEventPredictionQuestionGenerator
tools.description: Generates a set of personalized adverse‐event prediction questions for a given disease and drug, across multiple patient subgroups.

disease_name.description: The name of the disease or condition

tools.name: AdverseEventICDMapper
tools.description: Extracts adverse events from narrative clinical or pharmacovigilance text and maps each event to the most specific ICD-10-CM code.

source_text.description: Unstructured narrative text that may mention adverse events.

tools.name: AdverseEventPredictionQuestionGeneratorWithContext
tools.description: Generates a set of personalized adverse‐event prediction questions for a given disease and drug, incorporating additional context information such as patient medical history, clinical findings, or research data.

disease_name.description: The name of the disease or condition

context_information.description: Additional context information such as patient medical history, clinical findings, research data, or other relevant background information that should inform the adverse event prediction questions

tools.name: DailyMed_search_spls
tools.description: Search SPL list using multiple filter conditions (drug_name/ndc/rxcui/setid) and return metadata + data array.

drug_name.description: Generic or brand name of the drug, e.g., 'TAMSULOSIN HYDROCHLORIDE'.

setid.description: Set ID corresponding to the SPL.

pagesize.description: Number of items per page, maximum 100, default 100.

tools.name: DailyMed_get_spl_by_setid
tools.description: Get complete label corresponding to SPL Set ID, returns content in XML or JSON format.

tools.name: HPA_search_genes_by_query
tools.description: Search for matching genes by gene name, keywords, or cell line names and return Ensembl ID list. This is the entry tool for many HPA query workflows.

search_query.description: Gene name, alias, keyword, or cell line name to search for, e.g., 'EGFR', 'TP53', or 'MCF7'.

tools.name: HPA_get_comparative_expression_by_gene_and_cellline
tools.description: Compare the expression level differences of a gene between a specific cell line and healthy tissues using gene name and cell line name.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'BRCA1', 'EGFR', etc.

cell_line.description: Cell line name, supported cell lines include: ishikawa, hela, mcf7, a549, hepg2, jurkat, pc3, rh30, siha, u251.

tools.name: HPA_get_disease_expression_by_gene_tissue_disease
tools.description: Compare the expression level of a gene in specific disease state versus healthy state using gene name, tissue type, and disease name.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'BRCA1', 'KRAS', etc.

tissue_type.description: Tissue type, e.g., 'brain', 'breast', 'colon', 'lung', etc., optional parameter.

disease_name.description: Disease name, supported diseases include: brain_cancer, breast_cancer, colon_cancer, lung_cancer, liver_cancer, prostate_cancer, kidney_cancer, pancreatic_cancer, stomach_cancer, ovarian_cancer.

tools.name: HPA_get_biological_processes_by_gene
tools.description: Get biological process information for a gene, with special focus on key processes like apoptosis, cell cycle, etc.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'CDK1', 'CASP3', etc.

filter_processes.description: Whether to filter and focus on specific biological processes (apoptosis, cell cycle, transcription regulation, etc.), defaults to true.

tools.name: HPA_get_comprehensive_gene_details_by_ensembl_id
tools.description: Get detailed in-depth information from gene page using Ensembl Gene ID, including image URLs, antibody data, protein expression, and comprehensive information. This is the core tool for retrieving all images (tissue immunohistochemistry, subcellular immunofluorescence).

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID, e.g., 'ENSG00000064787' (BCAS1), 'ENSG00000141510' (TP53), etc. Usually obtained through HPA_search_genes_by_query tool.

include_images.description: Whether to include image URL information (immunofluorescence, cell line images, etc.), defaults to true.

include_antibodies.description: Whether to include detailed antibody information (validation status, Western blot data, etc.), defaults to true.

include_expression.description: Whether to include detailed expression data (tissue specificity, subcellular localization, etc.), defaults to true.

tools.name: HPA_get_cancer_prognostics_by_gene
tools.description: Retrieve prognostic value of a gene across various cancer types, indicating if its expression level correlates with patient survival outcomes.

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID of the gene to check, e.g., 'ENSG00000141510' for TP53, 'ENSG00000012048' for BRCA1.

tools.name: HPA_get_protein_interactions_by_gene
tools.description: Fetch known protein-protein interaction partners for a given gene from Human Protein Atlas database.

tools.name: HPA_get_rna_expression_in_specific_tissues
tools.description: Query RNA expression levels (nTPM) for a specific gene in one or more user-specified tissues with precise tissue matching.

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID for the gene, e.g., 'ENSG00000141510' for TP53.

tissue_names.description: List of tissue names to query, e.g., ['brain', 'liver', 'heart muscle', 'kidney']. Case-insensitive matching is supported.

tools.name: HPA_get_contextual_biological_process_analysis
tools.description: Analyze a gene's biological processes in the context of a specific tissue or cell line by integrating functional annotation with expression data to determine functional relevance.

context_name.description: Name of the tissue or cell line to provide context, e.g., 'brain', 'liver', 'hela', 'mcf7'.

tools.name: HPA_get_rna_expression_by_source
tools.description: Get RNA expression level (nTPM) for a gene in a specific biological source using optimized columns parameter. Supports tissue, blood, brain, and single_cell source types with comprehensive source mappings.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'GFAP', 'TP53', 'BRCA1', etc.

source_type.description: The type of biological source. Choose from: 'tissue', 'blood', 'brain', 'single_cell'.

source_name.description: The specific name of the biological source, e.g., 'liver', 'heart_muscle', 't_cell', 'hepatocytes', 'cerebellum'. Must be a valid name from the comprehensive HPA columns mapping.

tools.name: HPA_get_subcellular_location
tools.description: Get annotated subcellular locations for a protein using optimized columns parameter. Retrieves both main and additional subcellular locations efficiently.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'CCNB1', 'TP53', 'EGFR', etc.

tools.name: HPA_get_gene_basic_info_by_ensembl_id
tools.description: Get gene basic information and expression data from Human Protein Atlas using Ensembl Gene ID. Enhanced version now uses efficient JSON API.

tools.name: HPA_get_gene_tsv_data_by_ensembl_id
tools.description: Get detailed gene data in TSV format from Human Protein Atlas using Ensembl Gene ID (backward compatibility tool).

tools.name: Reactome_get_pathway_reactions
tools.description: Query all Reactions contained under a Pathway using Pathway Stable ID. This is currently the only working Reactome tool.

tools.name: PubChem_get_associated_patents_by_CID
tools.description: Get a list of patents associated with a specific compound CID.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

tools.name: PubChem_get_compound_properties_by_CID
tools.description: Get a set of specified molecular properties through CID (Compound ID), such as molecular weight, IUPAC name, Canonical SMILES.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

tools.name: PubChem_get_CID_by_compound_name
tools.description: Retrieve corresponding CID list (IdentifierList) by chemical name.

tools.name: PubChem_get_CID_by_SMILES
tools.description: Retrieve corresponding CID list by SMILES string.

tools.name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_substructure
tools.description: Search for all CIDs in PubChem that contain the given substructure (SMILES).

smiles.description: SMILES of substructure (e.g., "c1ccccc1" corresponds to benzene ring).

tools.name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_similarity
tools.description: Search by similarity (Tanimoto coefficient), returns CID list of compounds with similarity above threshold to given SMILES molecule, returns no more than 10 CIDs (MaxRecords=10)

threshold.description: Similarity threshold (between 0 and 1), e.g., 0.9 means 90% similarity.

tools.name: PubChem_get_compound_2D_image_by_CID
tools.description: Get 2D structure image (PNG format) of compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to get image for, e.g., 2244.

tools.name: PubChem_get_compound_synonyms_by_CID
tools.description: Get complete list of synonyms for compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to query synonyms for, e.g., 2244.

tools.name: PubChem_get_compound_xrefs_by_CID
tools.description: Get external references (XRefs) for compound by CID, including links to ChEBI, DrugBank, KEGG, etc.

cid.description: Compound ID to query external references for, e.g., 2244.

xref_types.description: List of external database types to query, e.g., ["RegistryID", "RN", "PubMedID"].

tools.name: MedlinePlus_search_topics_by_keyword
tools.description: Search for relevant information in MedlinePlus Web Service by keyword across health topics or other sub-libraries (such as drugs, genetics, etc.).

term.description: Search keyword, e.g., "diabetes", needs to be URL encoded before passing.

db.description: Specify the database to search, e.g., healthTopics (English health topics), healthTopicsSpanish (Spanish health topics), drugs (English drugs), etc.

rettype.description: Result return format, options: brief (concise information, default), topic (detailed XML record), all (includes all available information).

tools.name: MedlinePlus_connect_lookup_by_code
tools.description: Look up corresponding MedlinePlus page information through MedlinePlus Connect Web Service using clinical/drug/test codes (such as ICD-10 CM, RXCUI, LOINC, etc.), supports JSON or XML format return.

c.description: Specific code value to query, e.g., "E11.9" (ICD-10 CM) or "637188" (RXCUI).

dn.description: Optional, descriptive name (English) corresponding to the code, for drugs can fill in "Chantix 0.5 MG Oral Tablet", can improve matching accuracy.

language.description: Return information language, "en" for English, "es" for Spanish, default "en".

tools.name: MedlinePlus_get_genetics_condition_by_name
tools.description: Get detailed information from MedlinePlus Genetics corresponding to genetic condition name, supports JSON or XML format return.

condition.description: URL slug of genetic condition, e.g., "alzheimer-disease", must match MedlinePlus page path.

tools.name: MedlinePlus_get_genetics_gene_by_name
tools.description: Get detailed information from MedlinePlus Genetics corresponding to gene name, supports JSON or XML format return.

gene.description: URL slug of gene name, e.g., "BRCA1", must match MedlinePlus page path.

tools.name: MedlinePlus_get_genetics_index
tools.description: Download index file (XML) of all genetics entries in MedlinePlus, get complete list in one call.

tools.name: UniProt_get_entry_by_accession
tools.description: Get the complete JSON entry for a specified UniProtKB accession.

tools.name: UniProt_get_function_by_accession
tools.description: Extract functional annotations from UniProtKB entry (Comment type = FUNCTION).

tools.name: UniProt_get_recommended_name_by_accession
tools.description: Extract the recommended protein name (recommendedName) from UniProtKB entry.

tools.name: UniProt_get_alternative_names_by_accession
tools.description: Extract all alternative names (alternativeNames) from UniProtKB entry.

tools.name: UniProt_get_organism_by_accession
tools.description: Extract the organism scientific name from UniProtKB entry.

tools.name: UniProt_get_subcellular_location_by_accession
tools.description: Extract subcellular localization annotations from UniProtKB entry (Comment type = SUBCELLULAR LOCATION).

tools.name: UniProt_get_disease_variants_by_accession
tools.description: Extract all variants (feature type = VARIANT) and their related annotations from UniProtKB entry.

tools.name: UniProt_get_ptm_processing_by_accession
tools.description: Extract all PTM and processing sites from UniProtKB entry (feature type = MODIFIED RESIDUE or SIGNAL, etc.).

tools.name: GO_search_terms
tools.description: Searches for Gene Ontology (GO) terms by a keyword using the GOlr search engine. Returns GO terms and related biological entities.

query.description: The keyword to search for, e.g., 'apoptosis' or 'kinase activity'.

tools.name: GO_get_term_details
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GO ID using the Biolink API, including definition, synonyms, and annotations.

id.description: The standard GO term ID, e.g., 'GO:0006915' for apoptotic process.

tools.name: GO_get_term_by_id
tools.description: Retrieves basic GO term information by ID using GOlr search.

id.description: The standard GO term ID, e.g., 'GO:0006915' for apoptotic process.

tools.name: GO_get_genes_for_term
tools.description: Finds all genes/proteins associated with a specific Gene Ontology term using the Biolink API.

id.description: The standard GO term ID, e.g., 'GO:0006915'.

taxon.description: Optional species filter using a NCBI taxon ID. For example, Human is 'NCBITaxon:9606', and Mouse is 'NCBITaxon:10090'.

rows.description: The number of genes to return. Default is 100.

tools.name: GO_get_annotations_for_gene
tools.description: Finds all GO annotations for a specific gene/protein using GOlr search.

gene_id.description: A gene identifier such as gene symbol (e.g., 'TP53') or database ID.

tools.name: DrugSafetyAnalyzer
tools.description: Comprehensive drug safety analysis combining adverse event data, literature review, and molecular information

drug_name.description: Name of the drug to analyze

serious_events_only.description: Focus only on serious adverse events

tools.name: ToolDescriptionOptimizer
tools.description: Optimizes a tool's description and parameter descriptions by generating test cases, executing them, analyzing the results, and suggesting improved descriptions for both the tool and its arguments. Optionally saves a comprehensive optimization report to a file without overwriting the original.

tool_config.description: The full configuration of the tool to optimize.

save_to_file.description: If true, save the optimized description to a file (do not overwrite the original).

output_file.description: Optional file path to save the optimized description. If not provided, use '<tool_name>_optimized_description.txt'.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of optimization rounds to perform.

satisfaction_threshold.description: Quality score threshold (1-10) to consider optimization satisfactory.

tools.name: ToolDiscover
tools.description: Generates new ToolUniverse-compliant tools based on short descriptions through an intelligent discovery and refinement process. Automatically determines the optimal tool type and category, discovers similar existing tools, generates initial specifications, and iteratively refines the tool configuration using agentic optimization tools until it meets quality standards.

tool_description.description: Short description of the desired tool functionality and purpose. Tool Discover will automatically analyze this to determine the optimal tool type (PackageTool, RESTTool, XMLTool, or AgenticTool) and appropriate category.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of refinement iterations to perform.

save_to_file.description: Whether to save the generated tool configuration and report to a file.

query.description: Keyword or keyphrase to search for in the patent application title. This field is required.

offset.description: The starting position (zero-indexed) of the result set. Default is 0.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return. Default is 25.

rangeFilters.description: Limits results to the date range specified for one of the following fields: filingDate or grantDate. Provide the field name, a space, and a colon-separated start and end value in YYYY-MM-DD format. For example: 'grantDate 2010-01-01:2011-01-01'

tools.name: alphafold_get_prediction
tools.description: Retrieve full AlphaFold 3D structure predictions for a given protein. Input must be a UniProt accession (e.g., 'P69905'), UniProt entry name (e.g., 'HBA_HUMAN'), or CRC64 checksum. Returns residue-level metadata including sequence, per-residue confidence scores (pLDDT), and structure download links (PDB, CIF, PAE). If you do not know the accession, first call `uniprot_search` to resolve it from a protein/gene name, or `UniProt_get_entry_by_accession` if you already have the accession and want UniProt details. For a quick overview, use `alphafold_get_summary`. For mutation/variant impact, see `alphafold_get_annotations.

qualifier.description: Protein identifier: UniProt accession (e.g., 'P69905'), entry name (e.g., 'HBA_HUMAN'), or CRC64 checksum.

sequence_checksum.description: Optional CRC64 checksum of the UniProt sequence.

tools.name: alphafold_get_summary
tools.description: Retrieve summary details of AlphaFold 3D models for a given protein. Input must be a UniProt accession, entry name, or CRC64 checksum. Returns lightweight information such as sequence length, coverage, confidence scores, experimental method, resolution, oligomeric state, and structural entities. If you only know the protein/gene name, first use `uniprot_search` to find the accession. For full residue-level 3D predictions with downloadable coordinates, call `alphafold_get_prediction`. For curated variants, see `UniProt_get_disease_variants_by_accession`; for predicted mutation effects, use `alphafold_get_annotations`.

qualifier.description: Protein identifier: UniProt accession, entry name, or CRC64 checksum.

tools.name: alphafold_get_annotations
tools.description: Retrieve AlphaFold variant annotations (e.g., missense mutations) for a given UniProt accession (e.g., 'P69905'). Input must be a UniProt accession, entry name, or CRC64 checksum, along with an annotation type (currently only 'MUTAGEN'). Use this tool to explore predicted pathogenicity or functional effects of substitutions. If you only have a protein/gene name, resolve it with `uniprot_search`. For experimentally curated variants, use `UniProt_get_disease_variants_by_accession`. To view the full 3D structure, call `alphafold_get_prediction`; for overall model metadata, use `alphafold_get_summary`.

qualifier.description: Protein identifier: UniProt accession, entry name, or CRC64 checksum.
